title: Predicting Mortality in Sepsis: The Role of Dynamic Biomarker Changes and Clinical Scores—A Retrospective Cohort Study

sample_size.source_quote: The study population consisted of 138 adult patients (aged > 18 years) who fulfilled the Sepsis-3 criteria

sofa_score_baseline.source_quote: The median SOFA score at the baseline was 5 (IQR of 4–7)

mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR not explicitly stated; each one-unit increase more than doubling the odds of mortality
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: CCI, NLR, SOFA_Base, age, WBC, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The baseline lactate level emerged as a particularly strong predictor, with each one-unit increase more than doubling the odds of mortality.

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: Each one-unit increase leading to a 73.5% increase in the odds of mortality
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: CCI, NLR, LAC_Base, age, WBC, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: Similarly, the baseline SOFA score demonstrated a substantial impact, with each one-unit increase leading to a 73.5% increase in the odds of mortality.

mortality_predictors.effect_size: Each one-unit increase in the CCI, the odds of mortality increased by 31.6%
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: NLR, LAC_Base, SOFA_Base, age, WBC, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: for each one-unit increase in the CCI, the odds of mortality increased by 31.6%

mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: a one-unit increase in the NLR was associated with a 47% increase in the odds of mortality
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: CCI, LAC_Base, SOFA_Base, age, WBC, PCT_Base, CRP_Base
mortality_predictors.source_quote: a one-unit increase in the NLR was associated with a 47% increase in the odds of mortality

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: PCT percentage decrease from baseline to M3 (PCT_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR 0.948; for each 1% decrease in PCT, the odds of 30-day mortality decreased by 5.2%
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline clinical parameters included in model
mortality_predictors.source_quote: the odds ratios of 0.948, 0.762, and 0.887, respectively... a 1% decrease in the PCT level corresponds to a 5.2% reduction in mortality odds

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: CRP percentage decrease from baseline to M3 (CRP_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR 0.762; for every 1% decrease in CRP levels, the odds of mortality are reduced by approximately 24%
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline clinical parameters included in model
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The odds ratio of 0.762 for the CRP_Delta signifies that for every 1% decrease in CRP levels over the first three days, the odds of mortality are reduced by approximately 24%

mortality_predictors.predictor_variable: LAC percentage decrease from baseline to M3 (LAC_Delta)
mortality_predictors.statistical_method: Logistic regression and ROC curve analysis
mortality_predictors.effect_size: OR 0.887; a 1% decrease in the LAC level translates to an 11.3% reduction in mortality odds
mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Baseline clinical parameters included in model
mortality_predictors.source_quote: a 1% decrease in the LAC level translates to an 11.3% reduction in mortality odds (1 −0.887 = 0.113)
Among the baseline predictors, the SOFA score exhibited the highest discriminatory power (AUC = 0.762), followed by age (0.730) and NLR (0.716)

The AUCs for the percentage decreases in the PCT, CRP, and LAC levels, from baseline to M3 (PCT_Delta: 0.843, CRP_Delta: 0.903, and LAC_Delta: 0.703), were notably higher than those for their corresponding baseline values (PCT_Base: 0.478, CRP_Base: 0.540, and LAC_Base: 0.689).
